variantPrompt: Your Min Stack tracks the minimum. Now track both the minimum AND maximum — can you extend your auxiliary structure?
title: Min Max Stack
description:
Extend your Min Stack to also retrieve the **maximum** element in **O(1)** time.

Implement `minMaxStack(operations, values)` that processes a sequence of operations:
- `"MinMaxStack"` — initialize the stack
- `"push"` — push a value onto the stack
- `"pop"` — remove the top element
- `"top"` — return the top element
- `"getMin"` — return the minimum element
- `"getMax"` — return the maximum element

Return an array of results for each operation (`null` for `MinMaxStack`, `push`, and `pop`).

**Example:**
```
operations = ["MinMaxStack", "push", "push", "push", "getMin", "getMax"]
values     = [[], [5], [1], [10], [], []]
Result     = [null, null, null, null, 1, 10]
```
hints: You already track the minimum with an auxiliary stack. What if each entry stored BOTH the min and max at that depth?
Instead of a separate min-stack, push an object {min, max} alongside each value.
On push: new min = min(value, current min), new max = max(value, current max). On pop: both revert automatically.
